sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: New state 29! Clicking (24, 50) caused significant changes. Let me check what happened.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed
before = last_transition.before_frame.ascii.split('\n')
after = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("=== Changed cells ===")
for r in range(len(after)):
    for c in range(len(after[r])):
        if r < len(before) and c < len(before[r]) and after[r][c] != before[r][c]:
            print(f"  ({r},{c}): {before[r][c]} -> {after[r][c]}")

# Check key colored centers
ascii_grid = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\n=== Key colored centers ===")
for r in range(len(ascii_grid)):
    for c in range(len(ascii_grid[r])):
        ch = ascii_grid[r][c]
        if ch in 'MO':
            print(f"  ({r},{c}): {ch}")

print("\n=== White pixels ===")
for r in range(len(ascii_grid)):
    for c in range(len(ascii_grid[r])):
        if ascii_grid[r][c] == 'w':
            print(f"  ({r},{c}): w")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Great! The purple square moved from (24, 50) to (27, 46). The pattern is:
- Click M center to move the object diagonally
- Purple square moved from (33, 44) -> (24, 50) -> (27, 46)
- White pixels appeared along the path
Now I have:
- M at (16, 49) - orange object
- M at (27, 46) - purple square (new position)
- White pixels at (25, 48) and (29, 44)
Looking at the image, I can see the purple square moved and there are white pixels. The purple ring seems to have reset (turned black).
Following the pattern, I should click the purple square M center at (27, 46) to move it again. But wait, looking at the image, the purple square seems to be in a new position, and there's a white pixel path.
Actually, looking at the changes more carefully:
- The purple ring at (16-20, 55-59) turned black
- The purple square moved from (24, 50) to (27, 46)
- White pixels at (25, 48) and (29, 44)
The pattern seems to be:
1. Click purple ring center to open paths and move purple square
2. Click purple square M center to move it
3. Click purple ring center again to reset and move purple square again
4. Click purple square M center again...
But I'm not sure if this is the right pattern. Let me try clicking the purple square M center at (27, 46) to see if it moves.
